channel.title: Travel on Jonathan Ma
channel.description: Recent content in Travel on Jonathan Ma

item.title: The Reason to Travel
item.description: I’ve become a frequent traveller ever since coming to Shanghai. My heart longs for every opportunity to wander and get lost in a foreign city. My body longs for every sensation of vision, smell and shock. It’s like a spell, cast on the lonely traveller. But behold: no longer is the traveller lonely. He was befriended by strangers from myriad of places: a soldier in service for the country, an office lady and a local from the land of ethnic minorities.

item.title: A Helping Hand
item.description: Here another lesson learnt: where there’s people, there’s conflict and unexpected hospitality. The conflict part comes when me and my roommate are urged to return the fees to the other two Hong Kong exchange students who both abruptly left the dorm the second night of arrival (as mentioned in the previous post ({% post_link race-with-time %})). Since the four of us signed on a contract, the two of them should not really ask for a refund.

item.title: A Different Kind of Lifestyle
item.description:
Intro

Maybe I am too used to the hustle and bustle back in Hong Kong. Living as a college student in Shanghai actually feels less stressful. Why, you may ask. There are several reasons, which relates to the motivation, the expectation and the value.

item.title: Race With Time...
item.description: Exchanging in Shanghai has always led me to a race with time on various occasions. The race has already started the moment I set foot on Shanghai grounds. We exchange students have been informed there will be a pick-up service a few days before the semester starts, so that the whole batch of us will be transported to the campus directly from the airport. We were told the transport will leave at 1pm, implying our flights should land around noon.
